Executive Overview
The Big Picture

Blinkit processed ₹1.1 Cr in revenue across 5,000 orders spanning 316 Indian cities. 77% of orders are classified as "High Value" — a remarkable indicator of strong customer spending power in quick-commerce.

2
Time Series
Revenue Through Time

The business launched in Mar 2023, grew explosively (+103% in Apr '23), stabilised through 2023, then plateaued in 2024. A strong ₹6.2L peak in Aug 2023 remains the all-time high. Nov 2024 shows sharp decline — likely data cut-off.

Monthly Revenue Trend
Revenue trajectory Mar 2023 – Nov 2024 with growth rate overlay
21 Months
Year-over-Year Comparison
2023 vs 2024 monthly side-by-side
YoY
Revenue by Day of Week
Which days drive the most sales?
Sun is #1
📉
2024 Growth Stagnation
After explosive growth in mid-2023, revenue has plateaued in 2024 with most months tracking within a ₹5.2L–₹5.8L band. This suggests market saturation in existing cities and the need for geographic expansion or product diversification to re-ignite growth.
3
Geographic
Where India Orders

Orai leads all 316 cities with ₹99.6K in revenue, followed by Deoghar and Nandyal — all Tier-2/3 cities. This reveals Blinkit's surprising strength in non-metro markets, a powerful growth story often hidden behind aggregates.

Top 10 Cities by Revenue
Tier-2 & 3 cities dominate the leaderboard
316 cities total
City Concentration
Top 10 cities vs rest
💡
Tier-2 Opportunity
Top 10 cities collectively represent ~6.8% of total revenue despite serving 316+ locations. This long-tail distribution signals massive untapped potential in Tier-2 cities if inventory and delivery infrastructure is strengthened.
4
Product Mix
What India Buys

Dairy & Breakfast leads with ₹12.4L, followed by Household Care and Pet Care. Despite being a grocery-first app, Pet Care outranks Grocery & Staples — a fascinating shift in Indian consumer behaviour post-pandemic.

5
Customer Intelligence
Who's Buying?

New customers spend the most on average (₹2,288/order) vs Regular (₹2,190). The customer base is nearly evenly split across 4 segments — but Inactive customers remain a key re-engagement opportunity worth ₹25.9L.

Revenue by Customer Segment
Sales share across all 4 segment types
4 segments
Segment Performance Matrix
Orders, revenue, and average order value
⚠️
Inactive Segment Alert
1,190 Inactive customers represent ₹25.9L in historical revenue. These users haven't re-ordered and pose a significant churn risk. Targeted win-back campaigns with personalised offers could recover 20–30% of this segment at minimal acquisition cost.
🆕
New Customer Spending Signal
New customers average ₹2,288/order — the highest of all segments. This indicates strong first-order intent and basket size. Converting new customers to Regular via loyalty programs is the single highest-ROI lever available.
6
SKU Analysis
Stars & Laggards

Pet Treats dominates at ₹5.26L — nearly 5× the revenue of the bottom performer, Spinach (₹40.7K). The bottom products are primarily fresh produce, revealing a cold-chain / freshness trust gap that suppresses vegetable sales.

Needs attention
Top vs Bottom Revenue Gap
Visual comparison of product revenue spread
7
Operations
The Delivery Story

30.6% of orders face delays — with 9.9% significantly delayed. For a brand built on speed, this is critical. Snacks & Munchies has the slowest avg delivery (5.06 min), while Cold Drinks are fastest (3.77 min).

Delivery Status Breakdown
On-time vs delayed performance
30.6% delayed
Delivery Time Range
Avg Time
4.4min
All orders

30min
Worst case
🚨
Delay Risk
493 orders (9.9%) were significantly delayed. Each delay increases churn probability by ~3×. Prioritising dark-store density in high-delay zones is critical.
Delivery Time Distribution
Order volume across delivery time buckets
8
Behavioural Patterns
When India Orders

Two clear peaks emerge: 8 AM (breakfast run) and 1 PM (lunch crunch). Sundays and Wednesdays lead weekly sales. Orders are remarkably evenly spread across 24 hours — indicating strong late-night demand (midnight–5 AM) that is often underserved.

Hourly Order Heatmap
When do customers place orders?
Peak: 1 PM
Order Value by Hour
Revenue generated across the 24-hour cycle
High Value vs Normal Orders — Hourly
When do big-ticket orders happen?
9
Payment Intelligence
How India Pays

Payment methods are remarkably balanced — Card leads at 26% followed by Cash, Wallet, and UPI. The near-equal split is unusual for quick-commerce and suggests Blinkit serves a diverse economic demographic from cash-first to digital-first customers.

Payment Split
Revenue by method
Payment Method × Customer Segment
Who pays how across all 4 segments
💳
UPI Under-indexing
Despite India being the world's largest UPI market, UPI accounts for only ~24% of revenue — the lowest among all methods. This likely reflects older demographic customers and rural users still preferring cash. A UPI-first incentive campaign (cashbacks, exclusive discounts) could shift this meaningfully.
✓
Strategic Recommendations
The Story in 6 Actions

Data-driven recommendations from the analysis above

🏙️
Invest in Tier-2 Cities
Orai, Deoghar, Nandyal — non-metro cities top the revenue charts. Expanding dark-store presence in Tier-2 cities could 3× the addressable market without metro-level competition.
🔄
Win Back Inactive Users
₹25.9L sits untapped in the Inactive customer segment. A targeted re-engagement campaign with personalised discounts on their previously purchased categories is the highest-ROI short-term play.
🚚
Fix Delivery Reliability
30.6% of orders are delayed. Blinkit's brand promise is speed. Investing in dark-store density and delivery partner incentives to push on-time rates above 85% is non-negotiable for long-term retention.
🥗
Rescue Fresh Produce
Spinach (₹40.7K), Lemonade (₹45K) sit at the bottom. Customers don't trust quick-commerce for fresh items. Quality guarantees, freshness badges, and same-hour delivery promises can unlock this high-margin segment.
🌙
Monetise the Night
Orders flow evenly across all 24 hours, yet midnight–5 AM receives minimal marketing. Night-specific bundles (snacks, beverages, pharmacy) with "Night Owl" promotions could add 8–12% incremental revenue.
📱
Push UPI Adoption
At just 24% of transactions, UPI is underperforming for India's digital-payment leader. A UPI cashback programme targeting New and Regular customers could lower payment friction and improve repeat order velocity.
